name: LAN scan for urirun nodes
summary: Opt-in, read-only LAN discovery of urirun nodes over netscan:// — the mesh has no auto-discovery by design (it only refreshes configured/enrolled nodes), so this sweeps the local /24 on the node port and returns the nodes that answer GET /health, as candidates for the user to add.
description: netscan:// fills the deliberate gap left by the explicit, key-enrolled mesh: there is no automatic node discovery, so a node must be known by URL before the host can reach it. netscan://host/lan/query/nodes runs a user-triggered, read-only sweep of the host's local /24 (or a given subnet) on the urirun node port (default 8765), probing GET /health concurrently and keeping only the hosts that answer as a urirun node (name, version, url, routeCount). netscan://host/host/query/probe checks a single host. It never connects, enrols or trusts a discovered node — it only surfaces candidates the user then adds (to host config / ~/.urirun/nodes.json) and which urifix can then resolve. Bounded by a short per-host timeout and a worker cap. Pairs with the host dashboard's Nodes view (a Scan button lists discovered nodes with an Add action) and urifix:// (which resolves a node URL once registered).
useCases: Find urirun nodes on the local network when one is not yet on the host's list.
Probe whether a specific host runs a urirun node.
Feed the dashboard Nodes view's Scan -> Add flow.

examples.title: Scan the local network for nodes
